articles.title: RFK Jr. is on a congressional hearing blitz, after a long absence from Capitol Hill
articles.summary: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. caps off seven budget hearings in as many days, the first time he has testified before Congress since September.

articles.title: Trump administration delays rule aimed at improving disability access in schools
articles.summary: Schools, colleges and other public institutions originally had until this week to make online content accessible to people with disabilities. Now, the Justice Department has delayed that deadline.

articles.title: How the airline industry could be impacted if Spirit Airlines is liquidated
articles.summary: Spirit has not confirmed a liquidation, though it has filed for bankruptcy protection twice. Experts predict rising fuel costs could push the company to close its doors for good.

articles.title: 'We can do better,' FAA head says of work to update U.S. analog air traffic system
articles.summary: The U.S. aviation system is being modernized — but FAA Administrator Bryan Bedford and other leaders say it will take more money to make the system more efficient and flexible.

articles.title: From the Himalayas to Newt Gingrich, the 'tree-huggers' prevail
articles.summary: On Earth Day, we take a look back at the rocky history of "tree-huggers." The term originated in the 1970s in the Himalayas and was later co-opted by American politics in the 1990s. Now, environmental

articles.title: With Virginia vote, Democrats gain edge over Trump's national GOP redistricting push
articles.summary: After Virginia voters weighed in on Tuesday, the redistricting set off by President Trump to help the GOP in the midterms has been countered and possibly surpassed by Democrats.

articles.title: Virginia Passes Gerrymandered Map to Help Democrats in Midterms: 4 Takeaways
articles.summary: Virginia’s approval of an aggressive gerrymander could give Democrats up to four additional House seats as they seek to win back Congress.

articles.title: Priceless 2,500-year-old golden helmet returned to Romania after Dutch museum raid
articles.summary: The ornate Cotofenesti helmet, which was stolen in January 2025 while on loan to a Dutch museum, was recovered last month.

articles.title: Mexico to beef up security at tourist sites after shooting at pyramids
articles.summary: Mexico's government is boosting security at tourist sites in preparation for the World Cup after a man opened fire at the Teotihuacan pyramids.

articles.title: Here’s Where the National Fight Over Gerrymandered Maps Stands
articles.summary: With Virginia’s vote on Tuesday, Democrats pulled close to even with Republicans, who may have further cards to play in the race to gain extra House seats.

articles.title: Here’s What the New Virginia House Map Looks Like
articles.summary: Democrats now hold six of the state’s 11 seats in the U.S. House, but the new map could allow the party to win 10 of them.

articles.title: Justice Dept. Charges Prominent Civil Rights Group With Financial Crimes
articles.summary: Republicans have accused the Southern Poverty Law Center, which is best known for investigating hate groups, of unfairly targeting conservative and Christian organizations.

articles.title: What I Saw Crossing Into South Lebanon
articles.summary: Our visual journalist David Guttenfelder traveled with displaced people returning to their homes in southern Lebanon, as a cease-fire paused the fighting between Israel and Hezbollah.

articles.title: Trust Trump? Iran’s Doubts Shadow Peace Talks.
articles.summary: Iranian leaders fear being burned again by President Trump, who tore up a nuclear agreement reached during the Obama administration after lengthy negotiations.

articles.title: U.S. Abruptly Rescinds Subpoenas in John Brennan Inquiry
articles.summary: The reversal came after the Justice Department replaced a career prosecutor with a loyalist to President Trump in the administration’s effort to charge the former C.I.A. chief with a crime.

articles.title: As Pakistan seeks to broker U.S.–Iran peace, citizens watch for gains at home
articles.summary: As Pakistan works to broker peace between the U.S. and Iran, many Pakistanis are watching closely to see whether the diplomatic boost will translate into improvements at home.

articles.title: What would happen next if Iran agreed to surrender its highly enriched uranium
articles.summary: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Scott Roecker of the Nuclear Threat Initiative about how the U.S. might go about moving highly enriched uranium out of Iran.

articles.title: U.S. Turns Up Pressure on Iraq to Distance Itself From Iran
articles.summary: Washington is demanding that the Baghdad government dismantle Iran-backed Iraqi militias that have been attacking Americans and U.S. sites there recently.

articles.title: Trump extends U.S. ceasefire with Iran at Pakistan's request
articles.summary: The extension was announced just hours before it was set to expire. The president did not provide details on how long the new ceasefire extension will last.

articles.title: U.S.-Iran ceasefire nears its end. And, Fed chair nominee faces tough hearing
articles.summary: The ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran is set to expire tomorrow. Peace talks between the countries remain uncertain. And, Trump's pick to lead the Federal Reserve faces a tough confirmation hearing

articles.title: Ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran ends soon
articles.summary: The ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran ends soon. President Trump says a U.S. delegation is going to Pakistan for talks, but Iran hasn't confirmed their attendance.

articles.title: Japan approves removing ban on lethal weapons exports
articles.summary: Japan endorsed scrapping a ban on lethal weapons exports, a significant change of its postwar pacifist policy, as the country seeks to build up its arms industry.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
